items.rationale: Seen as the endgame for blockchain scalability and for democratizing ZK so devs need no cryptography knowledge; rapid recent performance breakthroughs (Jolt 'fastest zkVM in the world', open source).
items.age_note: built steadily Aug 2023 (Lasso/Jolt papers) → Apr 2024 (Jolt Alpha); strengthening, not reversed

items.rationale: Unbundles Ethereum security from the EVM so developers can build custom decentralized services on Ethereum's validator set; framed as a 100x innovation unlock.
Rollup-centric roadmap plus data-availability sampling makes L2s dramatically cheaper, unlocking permissionless innovation and new apps on Ethereum's security base.
Blockchains as a counterbalance to centralized Big-Tech AI; permissionless compute/data markets (Gensyn) and crypto provenance as the only serious answer to deepfakes.
Cheap/instant global settlement (sub-cent, ~30% merchant savings vs card rails); long-stated goal now seen as practically arriving.
A 'living laboratory' producing richer governance models (Machiavellian DAOs, multi-client ecosystems) and finally a native legal wrapper for DAOs.
House view that tokens should align developers, coordinate economic activity and ensure credible neutrality — distributing to clients/apps — rather than acquire users or fundraise.
A multi-chain/rollup future is treated as settled; fragmentation is the key problem, so messaging and shared-sequencing layers that re-unify composability are prized.
Programmable on-chain IP/remix graphs enable 'multiplayer for IP'; NFTs reframed as community-owned brands and purely additive value creation, not just speculation.
items.age_note: NFT-as-collectible/ultra-scarcity framing of 2022 evolved into IP/brand framing by 2023-2024

items.rationale: Privacy treated as a prerequisite for mainstream adoption and for broadening DeFi/identity use cases — but it must be compliance-aware to avoid sanctions blowback.
Applying decentralization to physical networks — compute/GPU, energy, telecom, transport — to unlock idle resources and democratize access.
Dominant recent worry: purely speculative tokens with no protocol/art/game divert capital, deter great founders, arm hostile regulators, and damage crypto's legitimacy and long-term thesis.
items.age_note: casino critique present 2022-2023, sharply intensified Dec 2023 → Apr 2024 amid memecoin mania

items.rationale: Single largest economic drag on the sector; SEC seen as fostering uncertainty, expanding 'dealer'/'exchange'/'broker' definitions to attack DEXs and decentralization rather than make rules. Congress/CFTC favored.
items.age_note: persistent and consistent since 2022; unchanged through Apr 2024

items.rationale: Short/chunky lockups enable VCs to dump on retail; fixed (non-dilutable) allocations take tokens from founders; both push pump-and-dump behavior. House pushes 4yr/1yr-cliff standard.

Empirical 'flight to safety' across stablecoins amplifies run risk on weaker pegs; concentrated issuer power (and CBDC-style winner-take-all regimes) undermines decentralization. Algorithmic designs largely rejected.
MEV extraction is treated as a real tax on Ethereum users; cross-chain/shared-sequencer designs risk monopoly rent and re-centralization of ordering.
Privacy protocols that ignore illicit-finance concerns invite sanctions/bans that taint whole pools and set the whole industry back; design must mitigate, not flaunt, the risk.
Bad US policy drives development and oversight offshore; framed explicitly as a national-security issue (semiconductors/TikTok analogies) ceding ground to China.
A sufficiently large quantum computer breaks signatures (theft, PoS forks) and gives PoW miners an edge; post-quantum signatures are bulky. Researcher concern, ~15-year horizon.
items.age_note: stated mid-2023, not restated since; treated as long-horizon, defensible threat

items.rationale: Single entities controlling more than a third of stake raise security and credible-neutrality concerns; client-configurable parameters offered as mitigation.
items.age_note: raised Oct 2023; not prominently restated by Apr 2024

items.rationale: Richest developer ecosystem and composability; rollup+danksharding scaling roadmap; and SEC implicitly conceded commodity (not security) status by approving ETH futures.
Viewed as technically superior digital gold; renewed excitement that a VERIFY_SNARK opcode could make Bitcoin fully programmable and revive Satoshi's vision.
items.age_note: programmability optimism is the more recent angle (late 2023) atop long-standing store-of-value view

items.rationale: Treated as the transparent, 'safe-haven' stablecoin markets flee to in stress; not an issuer-yield security risk; central to the payments thesis.
Recurring concerns about reserve quality and lack of transparency; during stress, regulatory-arbitrage flows into USDT raise systemic peg/run questions even as it gains share.
The cautionary archetype: endogenous-collateral/algorithmic designs prone to death spirals; SEC Howey win over Terraform reinforced consensus against algorithmic stablecoins.
items.age_note: collapsed 2022; cited through Dec 2023 as settled cautionary example, not a live position

risk_regime.rationale: Structurally bullish: adoption/innovation metrics at all-time highs (State of Crypto Index, DEX volume, active addresses, mobile wallets) and strong conviction in ZK, restaking, L2 scaling, AI and payments ('the pieces are finally coming together', 2024-04-25; 'The State of Crypto Index is at an all time high', 2024-03-22). But tactically guarded — the team is openly alarmed that the memecoin 'casino' and hostile US regulation are setting the space back, so the optimism is long-horizon/builder-oriented rather than froth-endorsing.

notes: Recency-weighted to the most recent statements (Q1–Q2 2024 dominate). The corpus blends distinct voices: a policy/legal voice (Miles Jennings — regulation, decentralization, token-launch hygiene), a thesis voice (Chris Dixon — networks vs casino, crypto×AI), researchers (Boneh/Roughgarden/Zhang et al. — ZK, MEV, consensus, quantum, stablecoin run-risk), an economics voice (Kominers — platform competition, vampire attacks, IP), and an investing voice (Ali Yahya/Arianna Simpson — EigenLayer, Espresso, Jolt). Where these overlap there is genuine house consensus; I report it as such. Notable evolutions/reversals: ICOs went from a tolerated early mechanism to 'peak grift' (2024-04-03, 2023-03-01); NFT framing shifted from collectibles/scarcity (2022) to composable IP/brands (2023–24); the embrace of a multi-chain/rollup world hardened over 2023–24; the anti-casino stance, present since 2022, escalated sharply into a defining concern by early 2024. 'Decentralization' is the unifying lens across every excited and concerned entry — it is simultaneously the team's core technical thesis, its regulatory survival strategy, and the yardstick it uses to separate productive crypto from speculation. Conviction scores are intensity, not probability. Quantum and staking-centralization concerns are marked 'persisted' (stated mid/late-2023, not restated by 2024-04-30).
